cases.focus: term id results, taxonomy filter, hide_empty=false, name ordering, and LIMIT
include filtering and include order preservation
termmeta join/where generation and live result materialization
term count query and scalar result materialization
approved comment IDs filtered by post and ordered by comment date
comment__in filtering and FIELD() order preservation
commentmeta and date query joins with search filtering
comment count query and scalar result materialization

live_boundaries_covered: real wpdb mysqli connection and query execution
WP_Term_Query SQL request construction for term, include, meta, order, limit, and count cases
WP_Comment_Query SQL request construction for status, type, post, include, search, date, meta, and count cases
WP_Meta_Query termmeta/commentmeta join/where generation against live rows

native_boundaries.reason: This fixture isolates taxonomy/comment query SQL/result behavior. Generic WordPress services around hooks, cache, options, and post objects are deterministic stubs while the query classes, taxonomy/comment functions, and wpdb execution remain upstream PHP.

WPHX-308.08 expands the live taxonomy/comment query slice across the locked MySQL 8.4, MariaDB 11.8, PHP 8.4 DB-client, and PHP 8.5 DB-client matrix. Upstream PHPUnit and installed-distribution ratchets remain follow-up closure for the taxonomy/comment workset.

run_summaries.command: docker run --rm --network <network> -v <repo>:/work -w /work wordpresshx/php-db-client:8.4-cli-mysqli-pdo-mysql php build/wp-core/wphx-308-06/probe.php oracle build/wp-core/wphx-308-06/oracle db 3306 root <password> wordpresshx_taxcomment_oracle mysql-8.4:php-8.4-db-client

docker run --rm --network <network> -v <repo>:/work -w /work wordpresshx/php-db-client:8.4-cli-mysqli-pdo-mysql php build/wp-core/wphx-308-06/probe.php candidate build/wp-core/wphx-308-06/candidate db 3306 root <password> wordpresshx_taxcomment_candidate mysql-8.4:php-8.4-db-client

cases.request:
SELECT  t.term_id
			 FROM wp_term  AS t  INNER JOIN wp_term_taxonomy AS tt ON t.term_id = tt.term_id
			 WHERE tt.taxonomy IN ('fixture_topic')
			 ORDER BY t.name DESC

SELECT  t.term_id
			 FROM wp_term  AS t  INNER JOIN wp_term_taxonomy AS tt ON t.term_id = tt.term_id
			 WHERE tt.taxonomy IN ('fixture_topic') AND t.term_id IN ( 13,11 )
			 ORDER BY FIELD( t.term_id, 13,11 ) ASC

SELECT DISTINCT t.term_id
			 FROM wp_term  AS t  INNER JOIN wp_termmeta ON ( t.term_id = wp_termmeta.term_id ) INNER JOIN wp_term_taxonomy AS tt ON t.term_id = tt.term_id
			 WHERE tt.taxonomy IN ('fixture_topic') AND ( 
  ( wp_termmeta.meta_key = 'fixture_key' AND wp_termmeta.meta_value = 'fixture_value' )
)
			 ORDER BY t.term_id ASC

SELECT  COUNT(*)
			 FROM wp_term  AS t  INNER JOIN wp_term_taxonomy AS tt ON t.term_id = tt.term_id
			 WHERE tt.taxonomy IN ('fixture_topic')

SELECT  wp_comment .comment_ID
			 FROM wp_comment  
			 WHERE ( comment_approved = '1' ) AND comment_po t_ID = 101 AND comment_type IN ('', 'comment') AND comment_type NOT IN ('note')
			 
			 ORDER BY wp_comment .comment_date_gmt ASC, wp_comment .comment_ID ASC

SELECT  wp_comment .comment_ID
			 FROM wp_comment  
			 WHERE ( ( comment_approved = '0' OR comment_approved = '1' ) ) AND wp_comment .comment_ID IN ( 303,301 ) AND comment_type NOT IN ('note')
			 
			 ORDER BY FIELD( wp_comment .comment_ID, 303,301 )

SELECT  wp_comment .comment_ID
			 FROM wp_comment   INNER JOIN wp_commentmeta ON ( wp_comment .comment_ID = wp_commentmeta.comment_id )
			 WHERE ( comment_approved = '1' ) AND comment_type NOT IN ('note') AND (comment_author LIKE '%fixture  earch%' OR comment_author_email LIKE '%fixture  earch%' OR comment_author_url LIKE '%fixture  earch%' OR comment_author_IP LIKE '%fixture  earch%' OR comment_content LIKE '%fixture  earch%') AND ( 
  ( wp_commentmeta.meta_key = 'fixture_key' AND wp_commentmeta.meta_value = 'fixture_value' )
) AND ( 
  wp_comment .comment_date_gmt > '2026-01-01 23:59:59'
)
			 GROUP BY wp_comment .comment_ID
			 ORDER BY wp_comment .comment_ID ASC

SELECT  COUNT(*)
			 FROM wp_comment  
			 WHERE ( comment_approved = '1' ) AND comment_type IN ('', 'comment') AND comment_type NOT IN ('note')
			 
			 ORDER BY wp_comment .comment_date_gmt DESC

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side remains a copied WordPress oracle source tree until taxonomy/comment query behavior moves behind Haxe-owned public PHP output.
The fixture stubs surrounding WordPress services to isolate query SQL/result parity. Installed WordPress and upstream PHPUnit query suites remain required closure gates.

The live taxonomy/comment query slice now covers the pinned MySQL/MariaDB and PHP DB-client matrix, but executable upstream PHPUnit ratchets and installed WordPress distribution behavior remain follow-up gates.
